title: Task-level model selection driven by capability metadata
prompt:
FINAL GOAL: let each Kanban task run on a model suited to it, driven by the per-model capability metadata. Depends on the capability metadata layer + the Settings capability UI tasks.

A task should be able to target an appropriate model — at minimum a per-task explicit model override, and ideally a lightweight policy (e.g. cheap/fast model for simple tasks, strong-reasoning/large-context model for hard ones) that picks from the available models using their resolved ModelCapability (context window, reasoning support, cost, speed tier).

Respect existing constraints:
- Per-agent provider capability still gates which providers/models an agent can use (src/agent-sdk/kanban/provider-protocol.ts) — selection picks WITHIN that, never bypasses it.
- No cross-agent session sharing (memory 'no-cross-agent-session-sharing').
- Task agent/provider resolution already flows through agent-provider-resolver.ts and startTaskSession — extend that path, don't fork it.

Scope this as: (1) per-task model field on the task spec + wire contract; (2) UI to pick it in the task detail; (3) optional capability-based suggestion. Keep it minimal — no over-engineering (memory 'vault-no-overengineering-agent-rw'). Confirm the exact policy shape before building the auto-routing part.

Acceptance:
- A task can explicitly select a model; it's persisted on the task shard and used at session start.
- Selection is constrained to models the task's agent actually supports.
- web:typecheck + biome clean.
